audit_summary.summary_text: The skill is a legitimate Discord channel configuration tool. It writes a bot token to a local .env file, manages access policies, and provides user guidance. No malicious patterns, hidden instructions, or exfiltration mechanisms were detected. The skill's implementation aligns with its stated purpose.

vulnerabilities.title: Excessive Permissions - File Write Access
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The skill requests Write and Bash(mkdir *) permissions, which are necessary for creating the configuration directory and writing the .env file. This is expected behavior for a configuration skill.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Low risk - these permissions are required for the skill's legitimate functionality of saving configuration files.
vulnerabilities.remediation: No action needed - permissions are appropriate for the skill's purpose.

vulnerabilities.title: Unpinned Dependencies
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: No requirements.txt or package.json was found in the skill directory. The skill relies on standard library operations (file I/O, mkdir, chmod) which don't require external dependencies.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: No dependency files present in the skill directory.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Low risk - the skill uses only standard system utilities and file operations.
vulnerabilities.remediation: No action needed - standard library usage is acceptable.
